questions.question: Which bank has become the first Indian bank to be awarded dual international certification in ISO 9001:2008 & ISO 29990:2010 for Learning & Development?
questions.explanation: YES BANK was the first Indian bank to be awarded dual international certification in ISO 9001:2008 & ISO 29990:2010 for Learning & Development.

questions.question: Purvanchal Bank is sponsored by which of the following public sector banks?
questions.explanation: Purvanchal Bank is sponsored by SBI and jointly owned by Govt of India and Govt of Uttar Pradesh. Purvanchal Bank having its head office at Gorakhpur operates through the network of its 571 branches, in eleven districts of Purvanchal. Purvanchal, the north eastern region of Uttar Pradesh.

questions.question: When was Deposit Insurance and Credit Guarantee Corporation (DICGC) established?
questions.explanation: Deposit Insurance and Credit Guarantee Corporation (DICGC) is a subsidiary of Reserve Bank of India. It was established on 15 July 1978 under Deposit Insurance and Credit Guarantee Corporation Act, 1961 for the purpose of providing insurance of deposits and guaranteeing of credit facilities. DICGC insures all bank deposits, such as saving, fixed, current, recurring deposits for up to the limit of Rs. 100,000 of each deposits in a bank.

questions.question: Chit Fund Companies are regulated by
options: Ministry of Corporate Affairs, Government of India
Securities and Exchange Board of India
questions.explanation: Housing Finance Companies are regulated by National Housing Bank, Merchant Banker/Venture Capital Fund Company/stock-exchanges/stock brokers/sub-brokers are regulated by Securities and Exchange Board of India, and Insurance companies are regulated by Insurance Regulatory and Development Authority. Similarly, Chit Fund Companies are regulated by the respective State Governments and Nidhi Companies are regulated by Ministry of Corporate Affairs, Government of India. Companies that do financial business but are regulated by other regulators are given specific exemption by the Reserve Bank from its regulatory requirements for avoiding duality of regulation.

questions.question: Which among the following services/facilities are not permitted at White Label ATMs?
questions.explanation: In addition to cash dispensing, ATMs/WLAs may offer many other services/facilities to bank customers. Some of these services include: • Account Information • Cash Deposit (Acceptance of deposits are not permitted at WLAs) • Regular Bills Payment (not permitted at WLAs) • Purchase of Re-load Vouchers for Mobiles (not permitted at WLAs) • Mini/Short Statement • PIN change • Request for Cheque Book

questions.question: The MICR is a code of how many digits?
questions.explanation: MICR is an acronym for Magnetic Ink Character Recognition. The MICR Code is a numeric code that uniquely identifies a bank-branch participating in the ECS Credit scheme. This is a 9 digit code to identify the location of the bank branch.

questions.question: What is the target of Priority Sector Lending for scheduled commercial banks?
questions.explanation: Target of PSL for SCBs is 40 percent of Adjusted Net Bank Credit or Credit Equivalent Amount of Off-Balance Sheet Exposure, whichever is higher.

questions.question: The Banking Ombudsman Scheme is introduced under which section of the Banking Regulation Act, 1949?
questions.explanation: The Banking Ombudsman Scheme is an expeditious and inexpensive forum for bank customers for resolution of complaints relating to certain services rendered by banks. The Banking Ombudsman Scheme is introduced under Section 35 A of the Banking Regulation Act, 1949 by RBI with effect from 1995.

questions.question: Who is the first chairman of Banks Board Bureau?
questions.explanation: Former Comptroller and Auditor General (CAG) Vinod Rai has been appointed as the first Chairman of Banks Board Bureau (BBB) in Feb 2016.

questions.question: Credit Rating agency Standard & Poor's Financial Services LLC (S&P) is head-quartered in
questions.explanation: Standard & Poor's Financial Services LLC (S&P) is an American financial services company. It is a division of S&P Global that publishes financial research and analysis on stocks, bonds and commodities. S&P is known for its stock market indices such as the U.S.-based S&P 500, the Canadian S&P/TSX, and the Australian S&P/ASX 200. It is head-quartered in New York, USA.

questions.question: Which among the following committees is associated with the Bank Rating system in India?
questions.explanation: CAMELS rating is an international (primarily USA) supervisory rating system to classify a bank / financial institution's overall condition according to 6 factors. The six factors are represented by the acronym 'CAMELS'. As per the recommendations of Padmanabhan Committee (1996), the banks in India should be rated on a 5 point scale of A to E, widely based on international CAMELS rating model. The committee has suggested that supervision of banks should focus on defined parameters of soundness, financial, managerial and operational. Based on these guidelines, RBI has evolved the model for rating banks based on CAMELS. Each of the 6 components would be weighed on a scale of 1 to 100 and would contain several parameters with individual weightage.

questions.question: The Banking Codes and Standards Board of India (BCSBI) was set up on the recommendations of which committee?
questions.explanation: Under the recommendations from RBI constituted committee on Procedures and performance audit of public services under the chairmanship of SS Tarapore, Banking codes and Standards Board of India (BCSBI) was set up in February 2006. The independent banking industry watchdog is based in Mumbai, Maharashtra, it functions as an independent and autonomous body.

questions.question: Which among the following services/facilities is not permitted at White Label ATMs?
questions.explanation: In addition to cash dispensing, ATMs/White Label ATMs may offer many other services/facilities to bank customers. Some of these services include: • Account Information • Cash Deposit (Acceptance of deposits are not permitted at WLAs) • Regular Bills Payment (not permitted at WLAs) • Purchase of Re-load Vouchers for Mobiles (not permitted at WLAs) • Mini/Short Statement • PIN change • Request for Cheque Book

questions.question: RuPay card is the product of
questions.explanation: RuPay is an Indian domestic card scheme conceived and launched by the National Payments Corporation of India (NPCI). It was created to fulfil the Reserve Bank of India's desire to have a domestic, open loop,[clarification needed] and multilateral system of payments in India. In India, 90 per cent of credit card transactions and almost all debit card transactions are domestic; however, the cost of transactions was high due to monopoly of foreign gateways like Visa and Mastercard. RuPay facilitates electronic payment at all Indian banks and financial institutions.

questions.question: What is the personal insurance coverage for death and permanent disability under Kisan Credit Card scheme?
questions.explanation: Kisan Credit Card credit holders are covered under personal accident insurance up to Rs 50000 for death and permanent disability, and up to Rs 25000 for other risk. The premium is borne by both the bank and borrower in a 2:1 ratio.

questions.question: What is the minimum maturity period prescribed for Commercial Papers (CP)?
questions.explanation: Commercial Papers can be issued for maturities between a minimum of 7 days and a maximum of up to one year from the date of issue. However, the maturity date of the CP should not go beyond the date up to which the credit rating of the issuer is valid.

questions.question: Kisan Vikas Patra was first launched by
questions.explanation: Kisan Vikas Patra is a saving certificate scheme which was first launched in 1988 by India Post. It was successful in the early months but afterwards the Government of India set up a committee under supervision of Shayamla Gopinath which gave its recommendation to the Government that KVP could be misused. Hence the Government of India decided to close this scheme and KVP was closed in 2011 and the new government relaunched it in 2014.

questions.question: What is the minimum maturity period of Masala bonds?
questions.explanation: The minimum maturity period for Masala Bonds raised up to USD 50 million equivalent in INR per financial year should be 3 years and for bonds raised above USD 50 million equivalent in INR per financial year should be 5 years. In case the subscription to the bonds/ redemption of the bonds is in tranches, minimum average maturity period should be ⅗ years, as mentioned above.

questions.question: Selling a credit card to an existing checking account customer is called as
questions.explanation: Selling of banks products/services to an already existing customer - is the broad definition of what cross sell means. It can be selling an existing checking account customer a credit card or selling an existing credit card customer a mortgage. Banks have been using cross sell as a marketing approach to expand their footprint and also increase their customer base.

questions.question: What does 'SFMS' stands for?
questions.explanation: Structured Financial Messaging System (SFMS) is a secure messaging standard developed to serve as a platform for intra-bank and inter-bank applications. It is an Indian standard similar to SWIFT (Society for World-wide Interbank Financial Telecommunications) which is the international messaging system used for financial messaging globally.

questions.question: Federal Bank is head-quartered in
questions.explanation: The Federal Bank Limited is a major private sector commercial bank headquartered at Aluva, Kochi, Kerala.

questions.question: 'Faidey ka insurance' is the tagline of which of the following insurance companies?
options: Kotak Mahindra Old Mutual Life Insurance Limited
questions.explanation: 'Faidey ka Insurance' is the tagline of Kotak Mahindra Old Mutual Life Insurance Limited. Kotak MahindraLife Insurance Limited is a private Life Insurance company in India. The company is jointly owned by Kotak Mahindra group of India and Old Mutual of South Africa in 74:26 ratio respectively. The company was founded in 2001.

questions.question: Health insurance scheme 'Atal Amrit Abhiyan' has been launched by which of the following states?
questions.explanation: Assam government has rolled out 'Atal-Amrit Abhiyan' health insurance scheme which will provide coverage against several critical illnesses. The scheme will offer coverage against 437 illnesses in six disease groups, namely, cardiovascular, cancer, kidney, neo-natal, neurological conditions and burns. Both Below Poverty Line (BPL) and Above Poverty Line (APL) families with annual income with below Rs 5 lakh, are eligible for the scheme.

questions.question: Which among the following was the first insurance scheme implemented by the Agriculture Insurance Company of India?
questions.explanation: Agriculture Insurance Company of India Limited (AIC) offers yield-based and weather-based crop insurance programs in almost 500 districts of India. It is A Public Sector Insurance Company. AIC commenced its business operations from 1st April 2003 by taking over the implementation of the "National Agricultural Insurance Scheme" (NAIS) from GIC.

questions.question: Which of the following is a government-backed accident insurance scheme of India?
options: Pradhan Mantri Jeevan Jyoti Bima Yojana
questions.explanation: • Pradhan Mantri Suraksha Bima Yojana: is a government-backed accident insurance scheme in India. • Atal Pension Yojana: is a government-backed pension scheme in India targeted at the unorganised sector. • Pradhan Mantri Jeevan Jyoti Bima Yojana: is a government-backed Life insurance scheme in India. • Swavalamban Yojana: was a government-backed pension scheme targeted at the unorganised sector in India. • Rashtriya Swasthya Bima Yojana: is a government-run health insurance scheme for the Indian poor.

questions.question: Which of the following terms is not associated with 'insurance sector'?
questions.explanation: • Dividend: Policy owner's share in the divisible surplus of a company issuing insurance on a participating basis. • Estate: The value of all of one's assets at their death. • Probate: The legal and administrative fees required to settle an estate upon the death of the estate owner. • Glasnost: A policy that commits government to greater accountability and visibility, such as freedom of information laws. • Beneficiary: In insurance, the person to whom the proceeds of an insurance policy are payable.

questions.question: Which of the following is a Micro insurance plan of LIC?
questions.explanation: • New Jeevan Mangal: Micro insurance plan • Jeevan Akshay: Pension plan • New Jeevan Nidhi: Pension plan • New Jeevan Anand: Endowment plan • Amulya Jeevan: Term plan

questions.question: Star Union Dai-ichi Life Insurance Co. Ltd. (SUD Life) is a joint venture (JV) of Bank of India, Union Bank of India and Dai-ichi Life. Dai-ichi Life is a leading life insurance company of which country?
questions.explanation: Star Union Dai-ichi Life Insurance Co. Ltd. (SUD Life) is a joint venture (JV) of Bank of India, Union Bank of India and Dai-ichi Life, a leading life insurance company of Japan. Dai-ichi Life is the second largest life insurance company of Japan and is one of the top ten life insurers in the world. Dai-ichi Life was established in 1902 and is a recognized brand name across the globe. Dai-ichi Life is renowned for sound product knowledge, superior asset management skills, and strong operational capabilities to manage life insurance businesses globally.

questions.question: Which section of The Insurance Act, 1938 talks about the 'Nomination by policy holders'?
questions.explanation: Section 39 of The Insurance Act, 1938: Nomination by policy-holder - The holder of a policy of life insurance may, when effecting the policy or at any time before the policy matures for payment, nominate the person or persons to whom the money secured by the policy shall be paid in the event of his death.

questions.question: 'Rashtriya Swasthya Bima Yojana' has been transferred to Ministry of Health & Family Welfare. Originally the RSBY was launched by which ministry?
options: Ministry of Housing and Urban Poverty Alleviation
Ministry of Personnel, Public Grievances and Pensions
Ministry of Social Justice and Empowerment
Ministry of Women and Child Development
questions.explanation: The Rashtriya Swasthya Bima Yojna (RSBY) of the Labour and Employment Ministry is being implemented by the Ministry of Health and Family Welfare. The Labour and Employment Ministry handed over the RSBY scheme to the Ministry of Health and Family Welfare with effect from 1st April 2015. The RSBY, the health insurance scheme for BPL (below poverty line) families was launched for the workers in the unorganized sector in the FY 2007-08 and it became fully operational from 1st April 2008. It provides for IT-enabled and smart - card-based cashless health insurance, including maternity benefit cover up to Rs. 30,000/- per annum on a family floater basis to BPL families (a unit of five) and 11 occupational groups in the unorganized sector. The "Unorganized workers social Security Act, 2008" came into operation w.e.f 31st December 2008 and it encompassed ten social security schemes benefiting the unorganized workers including the RSBY.
